scalars.name: neMibVariant
scalars.description:
The variant of the SNMP enterprise MIB. This object will
        together with sysObjectID [RFC1213] uniquely identify the
        revision and variant of the enterprise MIB used by the NE.

scalars.name: neManufacturer
scalars.description:
Manufacturer of the system. This value is used to present
        in clear text the manufacturer of the system.

scalars.name: neDateAndTime
scalars.description:
Reports the current local time on the Network Element.
        It also allows to set the local time. However, SET
        requests may not be supported by all products.

scalars.name: neMemorySizeTotal
scalars.description:
The total amount of physical main memory contained in
        the NEMI.

scalars.name: neMemorySizeFree
scalars.description:
The amount of unused physical main memory contained in
        the NEMI.

scalars.name: neAlarmStatus
scalars.description:
The highest severity of all currently active alarms on the
        NE. Alarms that are not reported because of Alarm Report
        Control (ARC) or any other administrative state are not
        considered active. The values indeterminate(1) and
        notReported(7) are not applicable for this object. The value
        cleared(6) indicates that the NE reports no alarm
        (i.e. Alarm LED(s) off).

scalars.name: snmpWriteAccessRestriction
scalars.description:
This value is used in combination with the
        snmpWriteAccessTable for additional security for SNMP set
        requests. When the status is Disabled, SNMP set requests
        from any NMS (using the correct write community) are
        accepted (ie, the snmpWriteAccessTable entries do not
        apply).
        When the status is Enabled, however, only SNMP set requests
        from NMSs (using the correct write community) which are
        listed in the snmpWriteAccessTable are accepted.
        This object is read-only, meaning that it cannot be updated
        via the SNMP interface. The operator must configure SNMP
        write access permissions via a non-SNMP interface.

scalars.name: neEventsLogged
scalars.description:
This value is the sum of all notified events (trap counter).
        It is also the event number of the most recent event
        (neEventLogIndex) found in the neEventLog tables. It can,
        for example, be used to check if a notification (trap) is
        lost.
        The value is reset to 0 after a cold start. The counter is
        increased even if no trap sinks are specified.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeRequest
scalars.description:
The request to the software upgrade function.
        Request 2..6 are single step requests while requests 7..9
        are batch commands for common upgrade scenarios. Requests 13,14
        are defined for automatic install process for F7 platform.

        If the NE is the FTP server, the file must then have been
        downloaded to the neSwUpgradeNeDirectory on the NE using the
        'netadmin' account.

scalars.name: sourceIpAddress
scalars.description: IP address used as source IP address in FTP Client session

scalars.name: neSwInstallState
scalars.description: Software installation state.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeType
scalars.description:
Required procedure type to be used for software download. legacy(1) is the default value.
         This parameter MUST be set before any other parameters will be set for software upgrade
         procedure (for example IP address, user name, password etc.). revised(2) value corresponds
         to Revised Upgrade Procedure which uses new PGM format. This parameter is NOT stored in the
         database and will be reset to default after SNMP Agent restart.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeState
scalars.description:
The current status of the SW upgrade process.
        NOTE: All failures that are caused by malfunctioning
              hardware are reported as internalError(17).

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeServerAddress
scalars.description: The IP address of the external FTP server.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeServerLogin
scalars.description: The login name on the external FTP server.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeServerPasswd
scalars.description:
The password on the external FTP server. Read requests on
        this object will return a zero-length string.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeServerDirectory
scalars.description: The SW file location (path) on the external FTP server.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeFileName
scalars.description:
The SW file name (without path) on the external FTP server
        or the name of the file which has been downloaded to the NE.

scalars.name: neSwUpgradeTransferProtocol
scalars.description: The transfer protocol to be used.

scalars.name: neBackupRestoreRequest
scalars.description:
Run network element configuration Backup/Restore operation:

        - none(1):       No action (read only)

        - runBackup(2):  Save the network element configuration to
                         a file (write only)

        - runRestore(3): Restore the network element configuration
                         from a file (write only)

        The Restore operation does not overwrite settings which are
        required to reestablish contact to the NE via SNMP and
        Telnet.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupProtocol
scalars.description: Connection protocol to be used for remote backup.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupSrcIp
scalars.description: Source IP address to be used for remote backup.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupTimeStamp
scalars.description: Add or omit the time stamp in the backuped filename.

scalars.name: neBackupRestoreState
scalars.description:
The status of the current NE configuration backup/restore
        operation activated by 'neBackupRestoreRequest'.

scalars.name: neBackupRestoreFile
scalars.description:
The full local path of the network element configuration
        backup file.
        This file is generated during the Backup operation and is
        also used for the Restore operation.

scalars.name: neRestoreFileBackupDate
scalars.description:
The backup time of the current file to be used for Restore.
        If no backup is available, this object reports 8 zero-octets.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupSrvIp
scalars.description: Remote server IP as a backup destination address.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupSrvDir
scalars.description: Remote server directory.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupSrvLogin
scalars.description: Remote user login.

scalars.name: neF7AutomaticRemoteBackupSrvPass
scalars.description: Remote user password.

scalars.name: neAutoBackupConfig
scalars.description:
The configuration of the regular network element
        configuration Backup/Restore:

        - disabled(1):            No regular backup

        - autoBackup(2):          Regular backup to a local file
                                  is active

        - autoBackupAndUpload(3): Regular backup and upload to
                                  external FTP server is active

        The default is autoBackup(2).

        If this object is not disabled(1) all other auto-backup
        objects are read-only. I.e., all neAutoBackupServerXxx
        objects must be set before changing this object to
        autoBackupAndUpload(3).

scalars.name: neAutoBackupInterval
scalars.description:
The interval between two subsequent backup actions in
        hours (default: 24 hours).

scalars.name: neAutoBackupNextActionAt
scalars.description:
The sliding time of the next backup action (default:
        midnight local NE time).

scalars.name: neAutoBackupServerAddress
scalars.description: The IP address of the external FTP server for backup files.

scalars.name: neAutoBackupServerLogin
scalars.description: The login name on the external FTP server for backup files.

scalars.name: neAutoBackupServerPasswd
scalars.description:
The password on the external FTP server for backup files.
        Read requests on this object will return a zero-length
        string.

scalars.name: neAutoBackupServerDirectory
scalars.description:
The directory on the external FTP server to which the
        backup files will be uploaded. The file name will be
        generated automatically (containing NE IP addres and backup
        time).

scalars.name: eqpFwUpgradeRequest
scalars.description: Update, Install and Reboot commands.

scalars.name: eqpFwServiceImpairment
scalars.description: Service Affecting Change during FWP upgrade.

scalars.name: eqpFwNextEqpForUpdate
scalars.description: The valid address of physically available Equipment part.

scalars.name: eqpFwEqpType
scalars.description: The TYPE of Equipment and the MODE setting determine uniquely the number and allowed TYPE's of the provisionable dependent entities (plugs, interfaces, modules)

scalars.name: eqpFwNcuServerBusy
scalars.description: NCU SW download busy

scalars.name: eqpFwEqpServerBusy
scalars.description: Firmware package download busy

scalars.name: updateEqpt
scalars.description: Number of equipment which have to be updated

scalars.name: installedEqpt
scalars.description: Number of equipment is already installed

scalars.name: selectedFile
scalars.description: Filename

scalars.name: ncuActivationDate
scalars.description: Activation Date. Format: YY-MM-DD

scalars.name: ncuActivationTime
scalars.description: Activation Time. Format HH-MM-SS

scalars.name: ncuScheduledActivation
scalars.description: Enable scheduled activation of the standby PGM partition.

scalars.name: ncuScheduledDbRestore
scalars.description: Shows whether scheduled activtion will be a database restore or a new software activation

scalars.name: encryptionFwpFile
scalars.description: The name FWP for encryption capable modules.

scalars.name: neRestoreConfig
scalars.description: Shows whether the last activation was(will be) a database restore or a new software activation

scalars.name: snmpServerPort
scalars.description: The TCP or UDP port on which a server is listening

scalars.name: snmpProxyServerAdminState
scalars.description:
The Adminstrative State will be displayed in the GUI with full
         name values; it will be differently displayed in TL1 syntax according
         to TL1 display rules. The transaction into the UAS state requires
         a special destroy/delete function.

scalars.name: snmpProxyServerPort
scalars.description: The TCP or UDP port on which a server is listening

scalars.name: snmpProxyServerSynchroState
scalars.description:
This parameter is used to synchronize proxy table on GNE with destination NE's
         availability.

tables.name: neStorageEntry
tables.description:
An entry for one long-term storage partition contained in
        the NE.

columns.name: neStorageDescr
columns.description:
The product specific description of the NE storage
        partition.

columns.name: neStorageCapacity
columns.description: The total capacity of the NE storage partition.

columns.name: neStorageAvailable
columns.description: The free space on the NE storage partition.

tables.name: snmpWriteAccessEntry
tables.description:
Entries in the SNMP Write Access Table.
        The maximum of rows in this table is product specific,
        typically 10.

columns.name: snmpWriteAccessNmsName
columns.description: Name of the NMS.

tables.name: neEventLogEntry
tables.description:
Entries in the neEventLog Table. Enterprise traps are
        always logged; logging of Generic traps is product specific.

columns.name: neEventLogTimeStamp
columns.description:
The NE Date and Time when the event (trap) occurred. This
        object is used in the trap varbind.

columns.name: neEventLogIdentityTranslation
columns.description:
Translation of entPhysicalIndex/ifIndex or other identifier
        to a string used in the trap varbind following the timestamp
        varbind.

tables.name: neEventLogVarEntry
tables.description:
Entries in the neEventLogVar Table. An entry appears in
        this table for each variable in the varbind list of an entry
        in the neEventLogTable. For an event having no variables,
        no entries are found in this table.

columns.name: neEventLogVarUnsigned32Val
columns.description: The value when neEventLogVarType is 'unsigned32'.

columns.name: neEventLogVarType
columns.description:
The type of variable. One and only one of the value objects
        that follow must be instantiated, based on this type. The
        used types depend on the enterprise traps defined for the
        specific product.

columns.name: neEventLogVarInteger32Val
columns.description:
The value when neEventLogVarType is 'integer32'. Integer32
        is also used to represent INTEGER types.
        Otherwise, 0 is returned.

columns.name: neEventLogVarIpAddressVal
columns.description:
The value when neEventLogVarType is 'ipAddress'.
        Otherwise, an empty address is returned.

columns.name: neEventLogVarOctetStringVal
columns.description:
The value when neEventLogVarType is 'octetString'.
        OctetString is used for all string types, including
        DisplayString, SnmpAdminString and DateAndTime.
        Otherwise, an empty string is returned.

columns.name: neEventLogVarCounter64Val
columns.description: The value when neEventLogVarType is 'counter64'.

tables.name: neTrapsinkEntry
tables.description:
Entries in the Trapsink Table. Entries are created and
        deleted using the neTrapsinkRowStatus object. The maximum of
        rows in this table is product specific.

columns.name: neTrapsinkCommunity
columns.description:
Community String which is sent with an SNMP Trap to the
        Management Station. If no community is specified, the
        default (public) is used.

columns.name: neTrapsinkUserName
columns.description: A human readable string representing the name of the user.

tables.name: swVersionEntry
tables.description: Entries in the swVersion Table.

columns.name: swVersionActiveApplSw
columns.description:
Reports the active Application software version on the
        unit/module.

columns.name: swVersionInactiveApplSw
columns.description:
Reports the inactive Application software version available
        on the unit/module.

columns.name: swVersionActiveOperatingSw
columns.description:
Reports the active Operating System software version on the
        unit/module.

columns.name: swVersionInactiveOperatingSw
columns.description:
Reports the inactive Operating System software version
        available on the unit/module.

columns.name: swVersionNextBoot
columns.description:
Reports which software will be run at next
        boot, either the currently active one again
        or the alternative (currently inactive) one.

tables.name: neF7AutomaticBackupEntry
tables.description: Entry releated to SCU/Remote backup.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupInterval
columns.description: Schedule database backup interval.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupStartDate
columns.description: Schedule database backup start date.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupStartTime
columns.description: Schedule database backup start time.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupNextDate
columns.description: Next schedule database backup date.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupRunState
columns.description: Automatic Backup running state.

columns.name: neF7AutomaticBackupTimeStamp
columns.description: Add or omit the time stamp in the backuped filename.

tables.name: sonetSectionConfigEntry
tables.description:
SDH/SONET interfaces will have an entry
         in this table.

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigTimingSource
columns.description:
The SDH/SONET timing source for this interface.
         - internal: used in stand-alone, point-to-point topologies
         stand-alone (dedicated fiber operation)
         - loopTiming: used in point-to-point via SDH network and
         feeder topologies. The default is internal(1).

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigSignalDegradeThreshhold
columns.description:
Block-Error-Based Degradation Definition for SDH (standard integration period).
         Defined as percentage Background Block Errors (30% default) evaluated
         over a defined period (SDPER-RS).

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigSignalDegradePeriod
columns.description:
The measurement period in seconds used together with
         the sonetSectionConfigSignalDegradeThreshold based on the
         block error counting method.
         The valid range is 2..10, The default being 7.

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigTraceForm
columns.description: Byte-Length of Trace Compared to Expected

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigTraceExpected
columns.description: Expected Sec/RS trace. NULL TRACE implies that no trace comparison is made.

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigTraceTransmit
columns.description: Sec/RS Trace to be Transmitted

columns.name: sonetSectionConfigTimMode
columns.description: Detection of TIM Sonet Section Condition can be configured

tables.name: sonetSectionDataEntry
tables.description:
SDH/SONET interfaces will have an entry
         in this table.

columns.name: sonetSectionDataTraceReceived
columns.description: The received Sec/RS Trace

tables.description:
OTU interfaces will have an entry
         in this table.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigSignalDegradeThreshold
columns.description:
Threshold for OTU SM-SD alarm (threshold level 0..100%, default value 15 %).
         Ref. table 24/G.7710.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigSignalDegradePeriod
columns.description:
Measuring period for OTU SM-SD alarm: 2..10 sec) (bursty)
         Ref. table 24/G.7710. Default value: 7.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigPayload
columns.description:
The payload of the interface (inside a transport overhead).
         Relevant e. g. for OTN cards which wrap a payload into an OTU1/2 wrapper.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigStuffing
columns.description: Indicates if bit/byte stuffing is used in the transport signal.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigTraceExpected
columns.description: Expected SAPI part of the OTU trace (15 character). NULL TRACE implies that no trace comparison is made.

columns.name: otuSectionConfigTraceTransmitSapi
columns.description: The transmitted SAPI part of the OTU trace (15 character)

columns.name: otuSectionConfigTraceTransmitDapi
columns.description: The transmitted DAPI part of the OTU trace (15 character)

columns.name: otuSectionConfigTraceTransmitOpsp
columns.description: The transmitted Operator Specific part of the OTU trace (32 character)

columns.name: otuSectionConfigTimMode
columns.description: Detection of TIM-OTU Condition can be configured

tables.name: otuSectionDataEntry

OTU interfaces will have an entry

columns.name: otuSectionDataResultingTotalBitrate

scalars.name: cmAlmLastChange
scalars.description:
A snapshot of the value of sysUpTime when the alarm situation
            last changed on the node (ie. there was a raise or clear).

            This value is reset to zero when the agent initializes.

scalars.name: cmAlmIndex
scalars.description:
This value is assigned globally and sequentially to each new
             trap that is sent from the device.  

             When this value reaches a maximum of 65535, it is wrapped to
             the value 1.

scalars.name: cmAlmTestAlarmEntity
scalars.description:
This object is used to specify the entity for which 
             the test alarm is raised/cleared. This entity can be
             OID of a card(unit) or a port (Ethernet Access or Ethernet
             Network).
             The test alarm is the trap cmNetworkElementAlmTrap with
             condition type as testalarm.
             If not specified, the value of this object is 0.0.

scalars.name: cmAlmTestAlarmAction
scalars.description:
This object can be used to raise/clear the testalarm
             on the entity specified by cmAlmTestAlarmEntity.

tables.name: cmSysAlmEntry
tables.description:
An entry containing information applicable to a particular
             condition.

columns.name: cmSysAlmNotifCode
columns.description: This object identifies the severity of the condition.

columns.name: cmSysAlmAdditionalInfoObject
columns.description:
This object provides OID of associated entity that caused the alarm.
          If there is no associated entity, this value is 0.0.

columns.name: cmSysAlmType
columns.description: This object identifies the type of the condition.

columns.name: cmSysAlmSrvEff
columns.description:
This object identifies whether the condition is service affecting
         or not.

columns.name: cmSysAlmTime
columns.description: This object contains the system time when the condition occurred.

columns.name: cmSysAlmLocation
columns.description:
This object identifies the location of the condition. The value
         none(0) should be used if location is not applicable.

columns.name: cmSysAlmDirection
columns.description:
This object identifies the direction of the condition. The value
         none(0) should be used if direction is not applicable.

columns.name: cmSysAlmDescr
columns.description: This object contains a text description of the condition.

columns.name: cmSysAlmObject
columns.description: OID of the actual object that raised the alarm.

columns.name: cmSysAlmObjectName
columns.description:
Symbolic name of the managed object to which the condition
         pertains.

tables.name: f3EnvAlarmInputEntry
tables.description: A list of parameters to manage the the environmental alarm inputs.

columns.name: f3EnvAlarmInputDescr
columns.description:
This object allows SNMP management entities to provide description to the
          environmental alarm inputs.

columns.name: f3EnvAlarmInputCondType
columns.description:
This object allows SNMP management entities to provide the environmental alarm
          condition associated with the environmental alarm input.

columns.name: f3EnvAlarmInputNotifCode
columns.description:
This object allows SNMP management entities to provide the notification code
          for the environmental alarm input.

columns.name: f3EnvAlarmInputMode
columns.description:
This object allows specification of the environmental alarm input mode.
          When 'disabled', environmental alarms are not raised.  Value of 'alarmopen' 
          allows alarm to be raised when environmental alarm dry contacts are opened.
          Value of 'alarmclosed' allows alarm to be raised when environmental alarm dry 
          contacts are closed.

columns.name: f3EnvAlarmInputAlmHoldOffEnabled
columns.description:
This object allows enabling/disabling of alarm hold off for this environmental
          alarm.  When 'disabled', the standard alarm hold off (2.5seconds) does not hold, i.e.
          alarms are raised immediately when conditions happen.

scalars.name: f3StorageDeviceInternalSsdHealth
scalars.description: Indicates health of internal SSD on server card.

scalars.name: f3StorageDeviceExternalSsdStatus
scalars.description: Indicates status of external SSD on server card.

scalars.name: f3StorageDeviceWearoutLevel
scalars.description: Indicated wearout level of internal SSD on server card.

scalars.name: neLLDPParamsLLDPEnableAction
scalars.description: This object allows user enable or disable LLDP on all the ports in the NE.

tables.name: networkElementEntry
tables.description: A conceptual row in the networkElementTable.

columns.name: neStorageType
columns.description: The type of storage configured for this entry.

columns.name: neRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of neRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            neRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The neRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: neAutoProvMode
columns.description:
Autoprovisioning mode which controls automatic creation of cards plugged
             into the slots of Network Element.
             Supported values are off(1) and auto(3). When neAutoProvMode is auto then
             the cards inserted into the slots of Network Element will be automatically
             provisioned. When neAutoProvMode is off no auto-provisioning will occur.

columns.name: neName
columns.description:
An administratively-assigned name for the
              Network Element.  By convention, this is the node's
              fully-qualified domain name.

columns.name: neType
columns.description: Type of Network Element.

columns.name: neContact
columns.description:
The textual identification of the contact person
              for the Network Element, together with information
              on how to contact this person.

columns.name: neLocation
columns.description:
The physical location of this node (e.g.,
              `telephone closet, 3rd floor').

columns.name: neDescription
columns.description:
A textual description of the Network Element.  This value
              should include the full name and version
              identification of the system's hardware type,
              software operating-system, and networking
              software.  It is mandatory that this only contain
              printable ASCII characters.

columns.name: neCmdPromptPrefix
columns.description:
User specified command prompt prefix, used by the CLI, 
          at the Network Element level.

columns.name: neAccepted
columns.description:
Indicates whether or not the Network Element is accepted or not.
               When the system's autoProvMode(CM-SYSTEM-MIB) is 'confirm',
               NetworkElement's are discovered with this object as 'false'.
               User must invoke the 'accept' action on neProvAction object
               to accept the NetworkElement.

columns.name: neFromPort
columns.description:
The Network Element port to which this 
          Network Element is connected.

columns.name: bits16PortProtectedCardRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of bits16PortProtectedCardRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            bits16PortProtectedCardRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).

            value of active(1).

            The bits16PortProtectedCardRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

         reset signal is held; When force offline is disabled, reset signal is released.

columns.name: stuHighPerCardRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of stuHighPerCardRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            stuRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The stuHighPerCardRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: stuHighPerCardForceOffLineAction
columns.description:
On some condition, user wishes to make one card out of service 
         and hold the reset signal of the card via AMI. When force offline is enabled, 
         reset signal is held; When force offline is disabled, reset signal is released.

tables.name: nemiEntry
tables.description: A conceptual row in the nemiTable.

columns.name: nemiEntityIndex
columns.description: Entity Index from ENTITY-MIB for the NEMI card.

columns.name: nemiForceOffLineAction
columns.description:
On some condition, user wishes to make one card out of service 
         and hold the reset signal of the card via AMI. When force offline is enabled, 
         reset signal is held; When force offline is disabled, reset signal is released.

columns.name: nemiFlashModelNum
columns.description: This provides the Flash Model Number on the NEMI card.

columns.name: nemiFlashFirmwareRev
columns.description: This provides the Flash Firmware Revision on the NEMI card.

columns.name: nemiAdminState
columns.description: Administrative State of the NEMI.

columns.name: nemiOperationalState
columns.description: Operational State of the NEMI.

columns.name: nemiSecondaryState
columns.description: Secondary State of the NEMI.

columns.name: nemiVoltage
columns.description: Voltage of the NEMI.

columns.name: nemiTemperature
columns.description: Temperature of the NEMI.

columns.name: nemiRestartAction
columns.description: Allows to perform specified action on the NEMI card.

columns.name: nemiStorageType
columns.description: The type of storage configured for this entry.

columns.name: nemiRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of nemiRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            nemiRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The nemiRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: ethernetCSMCardFineGrainedPmInterval
columns.description:
Allows specification of the fine-grained PM interval at the card level.
       This applies to all monitored PM entities. The default value of this
       attribute is interval-15min.  Valid values are interval-5min and interval-15min.
       interval-1day and rollover are not considered fine-grained intervals.

tables.name: mbGnssCardEntry
tables.description: A conceptual row in the mbGnssCardTable

columns.name: mbGnssCardEntityIndex
columns.description: Entity Index from ENTITY-MIB for this card

columns.name: mbGnssCardAdminState
columns.description: This object represents the Administrative State of the MB GNSS Expansion Card.

columns.name: mbGnssCardOperationalState
columns.description: This object represents the Operational State of the MB GNSS Expansion Card.

columns.name: mbGnssCardSecondaryState
columns.description: This object represents the Secondary State of the MB GNSS Expansion Card.

columns.name: mbGnssCardRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of mbGnssCardRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            mbGnssCardRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The mbGnssCardRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: mbGnssCardAlias
columns.description:
This object allows SNMP management entities to provide an 
             alias to the MB GNSS Expansion Card.

scalars.name: cmIpv6InterfaceMTU
scalars.description: This object describe the MTU for ipv6 address interface.

scalars.name: cmIpv6UnicastAddr
scalars.description: This object describe IPv6 Unicast Address together with Prefix.

scalars.name: cmIpv6UnicastAddrPrefixLen
scalars.description: This object describe prefix length of unicast address.

scalars.name: cmIpv6PrefixAdvertiseEnabled
scalars.description: This object describe whether to advertise prefix to link in router mode.

scalars.name: cmIpv6RAPrefix
scalars.description: This object describe the IPv6 Prefix to be dispatched to link in Router mode.

scalars.name: cmIpv6RAPrefixLength
scalars.description: This object describe Length of RA prefix.

scalars.name: cmIpv6StateAddrAutoConfigEnabled
scalars.description:
This object describe whether enable or disable stateless 
       address auto configuration.

scalars.name: cmIpv6DhcpEnabled
scalars.description: This object describe whether dhcp is enabled on the ipv6 interface.

scalars.name: cmIpv6DhcpRole
scalars.description: This object describe the dhcp role.

scalars.name: cmIpFlushARPCacheAction
scalars.description: This object allows user to initiate Flush ARP action.

scalars.name: cmIpv6PingInterface
scalars.description: This object allows user to specify the IPV6 Address related interface.

scalars.name: cmIpv6PingDestination
scalars.description:
This object allows user to specify the IPV6 Address to which
        ICMP Ping will be initiated.

scalars.name: cmIpv6InitiatePingAction
scalars.description: This object allows user to initiate ICMP Ping on cmIpv6PingDestination.

scalars.name: cmIpv6TraceRouteDestination
scalars.description:
This object allows user to specify the IPV6 Address to which
        Trace Route will be initiated.

scalars.name: cmIpv6InitiateTraceRouteAction
scalars.description:
This object allows user to initiate TraceRoute 
        on cmIpv6TraceRouteDestination.

scalars.name: cmIpv6ProxyNDPEnabled
scalars.description: This object whether enabled proxy NDP.

scalars.name: cmIpv6FwdEnabled
scalars.description:
This object whether NE works in router mode or host mode.
       Forwarding enable:router mode, forwarding disable: host mode.

scalars.name: cmIpFlushNDPCacheAction
scalars.description: This object allows user to initiate Flush NDP action.

scalars.name: cmIpPtpArpRtrvAction
scalars.description: If user want to get PTP arp from cmARPEntry, user need to configure this object first.

scalars.name: cmIpProxyARPEnabled
scalars.description: This object allows user to enable/disable Proxy ARP.

scalars.name: cmIpManagementTrafficDscpEnabled
scalars.description:
This object controls whether the DSCP value is modified for the transmitted
        management traffic whether it is locally generated or routed.

scalars.name: cmIpManagementTrafficDscp
scalars.description:
This object specifies the DSCP value and it is applicable when
        cmIpManagementTrafficDscpEnabled is enabled.

scalars.name: cmIpManagementTrafficBridgingInterface
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify the name of
        the IP interface the IP address of which shall be used as the
        IP address of the Management Bridge.

scalars.name: cmIpManagementTrafficBridgingIpv4Gateway
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify the IPv4 default gateway 
        for the Management Bridge.

scalars.name: cmIpManagementTrafficBridgingIpv6Gateway
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify the IPv6 default gateway 
        for the Management Bridge.

scalars.name: cmIpTraceRouteInterface
scalars.description: This object allows user to specify the IPV4 Address related interface.

scalars.name: cmIpv6TraceRouteInterface
scalars.description: This object allows user to specify the IPV6 Address related interface.

scalars.name: cmIpPingDestination
scalars.description:
This object allows user to specify the IP Address to which
        ICMP Ping will be initiated.

scalars.name: cmIpInitiatePingAction
scalars.description: This object allows user to initiate ICMP Ping on cmIpPingDestination.

scalars.name: cmIpPingResult
scalars.description: This object provides the results of a previous ICMP Ping Action.

scalars.name: cmIpTraceRouteDestination
scalars.description:
This object allows user to specify the IP Address to which
        Trace Route will be initiated.

scalars.name: cmIpInitiateTraceRouteAction
scalars.description:
This object allows user to initiate TraceRoute 
        on cmIpTraceRouteDestination.

scalars.name: cmIpTraceRouteResult
scalars.description: This object provides the results of a previous TraceRoute Action.

scalars.name: cmIpSNMPv1InterfaceName
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify the IP interface name,
        which will be used in the SNMPv1 TRAP PDUs as the Agent's IP Address.

scalars.name: cmIpSourceAddressType
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify whether the System IP
        Address or the Outgoing IP Interface's Address should be used 
        in outgoing IP PDUs.

scalars.name: cmIpSourceAddressInterfaceName
scalars.description:
This object allows the user to specify the IP interface name when
        cmIpSourceAddressType is 'systemipaddr'.  The IP Address associated
        with the IP interface name is set as the source IP Address in
        outgoing PDUs.

tables.name: cmIpInterfaceEntry
tables.description: Allows management of IP Interfaces.

columns.name: cmIpInterface

columns.name: cmIpInterfaceMask

columns.description: Whether or not DHCP is enabled on this interface.

columns.name: cmIpInterfaceMTU
columns.description: IP Interface MTU.

columns.name: cmIpInterfaceDhcpRole
columns.description: Specify the DHCP role on this interface.

columns.name: cmIpInterfacePhysicalAddress
columns.description: Physical Address associated with the IP Address.

columns.name: cmIpInterfaceRIPv2Enabled
columns.description: This object allows to enable/disable RIPv2 on the IP Interface.

columns.name: cmIpInterfaceDHCPClientIdEnabled
columns.description:
This object enables DHCP Option 61 on the IP interface.  This object
        is only applicable when DHCP client is enabled.

This object describe Destination IPv4 address.
             Only applicable for configured tunnel.

columns.description:
This object describe Source IPv6 address used to transmit 
            IPv6 packets on tunnel. Only writable for Configured Tunnel, 
            read-only for other types of Tunnels.

columns.name: cmIpv6OverIpv4TunnelLinkLocalAddress

columns.name: cmIpv60verIpv4TunnelLinkLocalAddrPrefixLen
columns.description: This object describe the prefix length for link local address.

columns.name: ipLoopbackInterfaceMask
columns.description: This object allows user to specify the IP mask of the interface.

columns.name: ipLoopbackInterfaceStorageType

            An entry MUST NOT exist in the active state unless all
            objects in the entry have an appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of ipLoopbackInterfaceRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes

columns.description: The type of storage configured for this entry.

columns.description: The status of this MgmtBridgeMac row.

tables.name: cmStaticRouteEntry
tables.description:
A particular route to  a  particular  destina-
       tion, under a particular policy.

columns.name: cmStaticRouteDest
columns.description:
The destination IP address of this route.
       This object may not take a Multicast (Class  D)
       address value.
       Any assignment (implicit or  otherwise)  of  an
       instance  of  this  object to a value x must be
       rejected if the bitwise logical-AND of  x  with
       the  value of the corresponding instance of the
       cmStaticRouteMask object is not equal to x.

columns.name: cmStaticRouteMetric
columns.description:
The primary routing  metric  for  this  route.
       The  semantics of this metric are determined by
       the routing-protocol specified in  the  route's
       cmStaticRouteProto  value.   If  this metric is not
       used, its value should be set to 1.

columns.name: cmStaticRouteRowStatus
columns.description:
The row status variable, used according to
       row installation and removal conventions.

columns.name: cmStaticRouteInterface
columns.description: Name of the IP interface for the interface static route.

tables.name: cmARPEntry

             this ARP Entry in the cmARPTable.

columns.name: cmARPInterface

columns.name: cmARPStorageType

columns.name: cmARPRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.
             The cmARPRowStatus object may be modified if
             the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1),
             notInService(2), or notReady(3).

tables.name: ipManagementTunnelEntry
tables.description:
A conceptual row in the ipManagementTunnelTable. Entries can
            be created/deleted in this table by management application action.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelAssociatedPort
columns.description:
This allows specification of the associated physical port on the device,
             to which the Management Tunnel is attached.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelName
columns.description:
This allows specification of a unique name which is also used as the name of the IP interface,
             to which the Management Tunnel is attached.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelType
columns.description:
This allows specification of the type of management tunnel, 
             whether it is a VLAN based tunnel or a MAC based tunnel.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelMTU
columns.description: This provides the MTU of the Management Tunnel.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelEncapsulationType
columns.description:
This allows specification of the encapsulation type of management tunnel.
             Only Ethernet encapsulation is currently supported.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelIpAddress
columns.description: This object allows configuration of the Management Tunnel IP Address.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelSubnetMask
columns.description: This object allows management of the Management Tunnel Subnet Mask.

columns.name: ipManagementTunnelVlanId
columns.description:
This object allows specification of the VLAN Id in the case of 
          a VLAN management tunnel.  This object is not relevant for a 
          MAC based management tunnel.

tables.name: cmIpv6StaticRouteEntry
tables.description:
A particular route to  a  particular  destina-
       tion, under a particular policy.

columns.name: cmIpv6StaticRouteMetric
columns.description:
The primary routing  metric  for  this  route.
       The  semantics of this metric are determined by
       the routing-protocol specified in  the  route's
       cmIpv6StaticRouteProto  value.   If  this metric is not
       used, its value should be set to 1.

scalars.name: ntpBackupServerAuthKey
scalars.description: This object specifies the authentication key for the backup NTP server.

scalars.name: ntpPrimaryServer
scalars.description:
IP address of the primary remote NTP time server. Value of 0.0.0.0 
             indicates the NTP server is not configured.

scalars.name: ntpBackupServer
scalars.description:
IP address of the backup remote NTP time server. Value of 0.0.0.0
             indicates the NTP server is not configured.

scalars.name: ntpType
scalars.description: Type of communication with the remote NTP server.

scalars.name: ntpActiveServer
scalars.description: Indication of which server is currently active.

scalars.name: ntpSwitchServer
scalars.description: Operation to switch the NTP Server.

scalars.name: ntpServerRoundTripDelay
scalars.description:
This object provides the round-trip delay (in microseconds) 
             to the NTP Server.  
             It returns a value of 0 if NTP is not enabled.

scalars.name: ntpServerPrecision
scalars.description:
This object provides the precision (in nanoseconds) 
             to the NTP Server.

scalars.name: ntpPollingInterval
scalars.description:
This object allows ability to configure the NTP polling
             interval in seconds.  Polling interval is the time 
             between successive NTP client requests to update local
             time based on time at the NTP server.

scalars.name: f3SnmpEngineID
scalars.description:
An SNMP engine's administratively-unique identifier.

                 Please note that f3SnmpEngineID differs from the
                 standard snmpEngineID (SNMP-FRAMEWORK-MIB) with 
                 MAX-ACCESS as read-write.

scalars.name: f3SysLastResetType
scalars.description: This provides the System Last Reset Type.

scalars.name: f3SysLastResetCauseType
scalars.description: This provides the System Last Reset Cause Type.

scalars.name: f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp1
scalars.description:
This provides the timestamp of the most recent abnormal reset.
           Note that the system keeps debug logs with 3 most recent 
           abnormal resets. 8 octets of 0 value indicates that
           there was no abnormal reset of the system.

scalars.name: f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp2
scalars.description:
This provides the timestamp of the second most recent abnormal reset.
           Note that the system keeps debug logs with 3 most recent 
           abnormal resets. 8 octets of 0 value indicates that
           there was only one (f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp1) abnormal
           reset of the system.

scalars.name: f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp3
scalars.description:
This provides the timestamp of the third most recent abnormal reset.
           Note that the system keeps debug logs with 3 most recent 
           abnormal resets. 8 octets of 0 value indicates that
           there were only two (f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp1, 
           f3SysLastAbnormalResetTimestamp2) abnormal resets of the system.

scalars.name: f3SysResetButtonControl
scalars.description: Enable or disable the use of the push button reset swith.

scalars.name: f3ConfigFilePassphrase

System firmware actions:

scalars.name: systemFirmwareActiveVersion

This provides ability to enable/disable the NETCONF over SSH

scalars.name: acceptDHCPBootFile

scalars.name: holdOffTimerEnabled
scalars.description: This is to control whether the hold-off timer is enabled or not to block traffic.

scalars.name: sshEnabled

This provides ability to enable/disable the Secure Shell protocol 
           on the system.

scalars.name: ftpEnabled

This provides ability to enable/disable the File Transfer Protocol

scalars.name: scpEnabled

This provides ability to enable/disable the Secure Copy (SCP)

scalars.name: serialPortEnabled

This provides ability to enable/disable the Serial Port 
           on the system.

scalars.name: httpEnabled
scalars.description:
This provides ability to enable/disable the HTTP protocol
           on the system.

scalars.name: httpsEnabled
scalars.description:
This provides ability to enable/disable the HTTP protocol
           on the system.

scalars.name: sftpEnabled

This provides ability to enable/disable the SFTP protocol

scalars.name: tftpEnabled

scalars.name: securityBanner
scalars.description:
This provides ability to manage the security banner text
           used for Graphical User Interface as well as CLI access.

scalars.name: serialPortDisconnectAutoLogOff
scalars.description:
This provides ability to manage the property of the system, which 
           causes Auto Logoff of the user session on the serial port, when 
           the serial port is disconnected.

scalars.name: securityPromptEnabled
scalars.description: This object allows management of the CLI security prompt.

scalars.name: ntpMode
scalars.description:
This provides ability to manage the Network Time Protocol (NTP)
           mode on the system.  Currently, the system only supports 
           'client' mode.

scalars.name: autoProvMode
scalars.description:
This provides ability to manage the Auto Provisioning Mode 
           on the system.  If the auto provisioning mode is 'off', auto 
           discovery is disabled.  Network Elements (shelves) need to be 
           manually configured from the user interfaces.  If the auto 
           provisioning mode is 'confirm', Network Elements (shelves) are
           auto discovered, however, they need to be accepted explicitly from
           user interfaces to be managed.  If the auto provisioning mode
           is 'auto', Network Elements (shelves) are auto discovered and
           auto provisioned, as permanent, in the system.

scalars.name: sysTimeOfDayType
scalars.description:
This object provides ability to configure System Time of Day source.
          This can be local, ntp or ptp. 
          Before setting sysTimeOfDayType the f3PtpSysTimeOfDayClock object must be
          set to configure PTP Telecom Slave object to be used as Clock source.

scalars.name: ntpServerConfigType
scalars.description: This object describe the ntp server configure type.

scalars.name: sysLogServerConfigType
scalars.description: This object describe the sys log server configure type.

scalars.name: sysUseUtcLeapOffsetEnable
scalars.description:
This attribute indicates whether to use the currentUtcLeapOffset 
            in Announce message from PTP to get UTC time from TAI based 
            timestamps in case we want DM/TWAMP timestamping based on UTC time. 
            This attribute only works when PTP/TimeClock is configured as source of system time of day.

scalars.name: syslogProtocol
scalars.description: This object describes the syslog protocol type.

scalars.name: databaseAction
scalars.description:
Initiates a configuration database action.  This object is
             write only.  Supported actions are:
             backup(1)          - Backup the saved configuration database.
             restore(2)         - Restore the database to the standby partition.
             activate(3)        - Switches the standby and active partitions and
                                  restarts the system.
             save-sysdefaults(4) - Save the database as system defaults.
             new-sysdefaults(5) - Restart the system and overwrite the
                                  old system default database with the
                                  factory default database.
             restore-sysdefaults(6) - Restart the system using the system
                                  default database. 
             restore-factorydefaults(7) - Restart the system using the factory
                                  default database.
             propagate-to-standby-nemi(8) - Propagate the running database 
                                  to standby NEMI and activate it.
             force-normal(9)    - Accept the database of the NEMI when the NEMI is in DB_maint status.

scalars.name: databaseLastSaveTime
scalars.description: This object gives value of the last database save time.

scalars.name: databaseActionPassphrase
scalars.description: Pass-phrase used to generate key for encrypting private keys.

scalars.name: softwareAction
scalars.description:
This object is write only.  Setting this object initiates
         the specified action.

         Supported actions are:
         install(1)          - Install software. 
         schedule-upgrade(2) - Schedule a software upgrade.  At the
                               scheduled time, the system will reboot and
                               load the software image stored on the
                               standby partition.  The scheduled time
                               can be specified by softwareUpgradeTime.
                               Also see softwareValidationTimer.
         cancel-upgrade(3)   - Cancel a scheduled software upgrade.
         validate-upgrade(4) - Indicate software as valid and cancel the
                               validation timer. 
         revert-upgrade(5)   - Revert to previous software image if it 
                               still exists.
         propagate-to-standby-nemi(6) - Propagate the running software release 
                               to standby NEMI and active it.

scalars.name: softwareUpgradeTime
scalars.description:
Specifies the date and time to perform a software upgrade reboot.
         If this object is set to a date and time in the past or has never
         been set, the upgrade reboot will happen as soon as softwareAction
         is set to schedule-upgrade(2).  This value becomes read-only when
         an upgrade has been scheduled.  Default value is 1-1-2000 00:00:00.

scalars.name: softwareValidationTimer
scalars.description:
Time in minutes before the system will reboot and revert to the active
         software after a software upgrade.  If set to 0, the timer is
         disabled and the software will automatically be validated after
         a software upgrade reboot.  This object becomes read-only when
         the software validation timer is active.  Default value is 0.
         When enabled, valid values of the timer range from 10 minutes to 720 minutes.

scalars.name: peerUpgradeStatus

tables.name: f3NtpAuthKeyEntry
tables.description: NTP Authentication Key Entry

columns.name: f3NtpAuthKeyNumber
columns.description:
This is the identifier used by the NTP authentication protocol using
         which the Client and Server identify the key.

columns.name: f3NtpAuthKeyType
columns.description: Authentication type, MD5 or SHA-1.

columns.name: f3NtpAuthKey
columns.description:
This is the key value and length depends on the authentication type used.
          It is 16 character printable string for MD5 excluding whitespace and '#' and
          for SHA-1 it is a 40 character hex-encoded string.

columns.name: f3NtpAuthKeyStorageType
columns.description: The storage type for this conceptual row.

columns.name: f3NtpAuthKeyRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of f3NtpAuthKeyRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            f3NtpAuthKeyRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The f3NtpAuthKeyRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: f3SnmpTargetAddrExtDyingGaspPort

When SNMP Dying Gasp is enabled at Card level, 
             this attribute provides information on the interface (physical port)

columns.name: f3SnmpTargetAddrExtDyingGaspEnabled

             used in the SNMP Dying Gasp TRAP PDU or not.

columns.name: f3SnmpTargetAddrExtDyingGaspActive
columns.description:
When SNMP Dying Gasp is enabled at Card level, in case of 
             multiple target addresses resolving to the same interface (port), 
             this flag indicates which target address is used for the SNMP TRAP PDU.

columns.name: f3SnmpTargetAddrExtBulkTrapsEnabled

Provides ability to enable/disable the Snmp Bulk Traps on

columns.name: f3SnmpTargetAddrExtLifetime
columns.description:
Provides a time after which target address
             entry will automatically be deleted. 
             SNMP packet received from snmpTargetAddrTAddress 
             resets timer to its original value.

tables.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtEntry
tables.description: A conceptual row in the f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtTable.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus
columns.description:
The administratively desired status of the local LLDP agent.

            If the associated f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus object is
            set to a value of 'txOnly(1)', then LLDP agent transmits
            LLDPframes on this port and it does not store any
            information about the remote systems connected.
         
            If the associated f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus object is 
            set to a value of 'rxOnly(2)', then the LLDP agent
            receives, but it does not transmit LLDP frames on this port.

            If the associated f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus object is set
            to a value of 'txAndRx(3)', then the LLDP agent transmits
            and receives LLDP frames on this port.

            If the associated f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus object is set
            to a value of 'disabled(4)', then LLDP agent does not
            transmit or receive LLDP frames on this port. If there is
            remote systems information which is received on this port
            and stored in other tables, before the port's
            f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtAdminStatus becomes disabled, then that
            information is deleted.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtNotificationEnable
columns.description:
The f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtNotificationEnable controls, on a per
            agent basis, whether or not notifications from the agent
            are enabled. The value true(1) means that notifications are
            enabled; the value false(2) means that they are not.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtTLVsTxEnable
columns.description:
The f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtTLVsTxEnable, defined as a bitmap,
            includes the basic set of LLDP TLVs whose transmission is
            allowed on the local LLDP agent by the network management.
            Each bit in the bitmap corresponds to a TLV type associated
            with a specific optional TLV.

            It should be noted that the organizationally-specific TLVs
            are excluded from the f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtTLVsTxEnable bitmap.
            
            LLDP Organization Specific Information Extension MIBs should
            have similar configuration object to control transmission
            of their organizationally defined TLVs.

            The bit 'portDesc(0)' indicates that LLDP agent should
            transmit 'Port Description TLV'.

            The bit 'sysName(1)' indicates that LLDP agent should transmit
            'System Name TLV'.

            The bit 'sysDesc(2)' indicates that LLDP agent should transmit
            'System Description TLV'.

            The bit 'sysCap(3)' indicates that LLDP agent should transmit
            'System Capabilities TLV'.

            There is no bit reserved for the management address TLV type
            since transmission of management address TLVs are controlled
            by another object, lldpV2ConfigManAddrTable.

            The default value for f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtTLVsTxEnable object is
            empty set, which means no enumerated values are set.

            The value of this object is restored from non-volatile
            storage after a re-initialization of the management system.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtStorageType
columns.description: The storage type for this conceptual row.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The f3SystemLldpV2PortConfigADVAExtRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtEnable
columns.description: This object describe the interface on Tx Ports whether enabled.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtStorageType
columns.description: The storage type for this conceptual row.

columns.name: f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtRowStatus
columns.description:
The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The f3SystemLldpV2ManAddrConfigTxPortsADVAExtRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

columns.name: f3SysAuthKeyType

columns.name: f3SysAuthKeyRowStatus

The status of this row.  An entry MUST NOT exist in the 
            active state unless all objects in the entry have an 
            appropriate value, as described
            in the description clause for each writable object.

            The values of f3SysAuthKeyRowStatus supported are
            createAndGo(4) and destroy(6).  All mandatory attributes
            must be specified in a single SNMP SET request with
            f3SysAuthKeyRowStatus value as createAndGo(4).
            Upon successful row creation, this object has a
            value of active(1).

            The f3SysAuthKeyRowStatus object may be modified if
            the associated instance of this object is equal to active(1).

Topology Link connects two Network Elements.  This object

scalars.name: entLastChangeTime
scalars.description:
The value of sysUpTime at the time a conceptual row is
            created, modified, or deleted in any of these tables:
                    - entPhysicalTable
                    - entLogicalTable
                    - entLPMappingTable
                    - entAliasMappingTable
                    - entPhysicalContainsTable

tables.name: entPhysicalEntry
tables.description:
Information about a particular physical entity.

            Each entry provides objects (entPhysicalDescr,
            entPhysicalVendorType, and entPhysicalClass) to help an NMS
            identify and characterize the entry, and objects
            (entPhysicalContainedIn and entPhysicalParentRelPos) to help





            an NMS relate the particular entry to other entries in this
            table.

columns.name: entPhysicalSoftwareRev
columns.description:
The vendor-specific software revision string for the
            physical entity.

            Note that if revision information is stored internally in a
            non-printable (e.g., binary) format, then the agent must
            convert such information to a printable format, in an
            implementation-specific manner.

            If no specific software programs are associated with the
            physical component, or this information is unknown to the
            agent, then this object will contain a zero-length string.

columns.name: entPhysicalSerialNum
columns.description:
The vendor-specific serial number string for the physical
            entity.  The preferred value is the serial number string
            actually printed on the component itself (if present).

            On the first instantiation of an physical entity, the value
            of entPhysicalSerialNum associated with that entity is set
            to the correct vendor-assigned serial number, if this
            information is available to the agent.  If a serial number
            is unknown or non-existent, the entPhysicalSerialNum will be
            set to a zero-length string instead.

            Note that implementations which can correctly identify the
            serial numbers of all installed physical entities do not
            need to provide write access to the entPhysicalSerialNum
            object. Agents which cannot provide non-volatile storage for
            the entPhysicalSerialNum strings are not required to
            implement write access for this object.

            Not every physical component will have a serial number, or
            even need one.  Physical entities for which the associated
            value of the entPhysicalIsFRU object is equal to 'false(2)'
            (e.g., the repeater ports within a repeater module), do not
            need their own unique serial number. An agent does not have
            to provide write access for such entities, and may return a
            zero-length string.

            If write access is implemented for an instance of
            entPhysicalSerialNum, and a value is written into the
            instance, the agent must retain the supplied value in the
            entPhysicalSerialNum instance associated with the same
            physical entity for as long as that entity remains
            instantiated. This includes instantiations across all re-
            initializations/reboots of the network management system,
            including those which result in a change of the physical
            entity's entPhysicalIndex value.

columns.name: entPhysicalDescr
columns.description:
A textual description of physical entity.  This object
            should contain a string which identifies the manufacturer's
            name for the physical entity, and should be set to a
            distinct value for each version or model of the physical
            entity.

columns.name: entPhysicalVendorType
columns.description:
An indication of the vendor-specific hardware type of the
            physical entity.  Note that this is different from the
            definition of MIB-II's sysObjectID.

            An agent should set this object to a enterprise-specific
            registration identifier value indicating the specific
            equipment type in detail.  The associated instance of
            entPhysicalClass is used to indicate the general type of
            hardware device.

            If no vendor-specific registration identifier exists for
            this physical entity, or the value is unknown by this agent,
            then the value { 0 0 } is returned.

columns.name: entPhysicalParentRelPos
columns.description:
An indication of the relative position of this 'child'
            component among all its 'sibling' components. Sibling
            components are defined as entPhysicalEntries which share the
            same instance values of each of the entPhysicalContainedIn
            and entPhysicalClass objects.

            An NMS can use this object to identify the relative ordering
            for all sibling components of a particular parent
            (identified by the entPhysicalContainedIn instance in each
            sibling entry).

            This value should match any external labeling of the
            physical component if possible. For example, for a container
            (e.g., card slot) labeled as 'slot #3',
            entPhysicalParentRelPos should have the value '3'.  Note
            that the entPhysicalEntry for the module plugged in slot 3
            should have an entPhysicalParentRelPos value of '1'.

            If the physical position of this component does not match
            any external numbering or clearly visible ordering, then
            user documentation or other external reference material
            should be used to determine the parent-relative position. If
            this is not possible, then the the agent should assign a
            consistent (but possibly arbitrary) ordering to a given set
            of 'sibling' components, perhaps based on internal
            representation of the components.

            If the agent cannot determine the parent-relative position
            for some reason, or if the associated value of
            entPhysicalContainedIn is '0', then the value '-1' is
            returned. Otherwise a non-negative integer is returned,
            indicating the parent-relative position of this physical
            entity.

            Parent-relative ordering normally starts from '1' and
            continues to 'N', where 'N' represents the highest
            positioned child entity.  However, if the physical entities
            (e.g., slots) are labeled from a starting position of zero,





            then the first sibling should be associated with a
            entPhysicalParentRelPos value of '0'.  Note that this
            ordering may be sparse or dense, depending on agent
            implementation.

            The actual values returned are not globally meaningful, as
            each 'parent' component may use different numbering
            algorithms. The ordering is only meaningful among siblings
            of the same parent component.

            The agent should retain parent-relative position values
            across reboots, either through algorithmic assignment or use
            of non-volatile storage.

columns.name: entPhysicalName
columns.description:
The textual name of the physical entity.  The value of this
            object should be the name of the component as assigned by
            the local device and should be suitable for use in commands
            entered at the device's `console'.  This might be a text
            name, such as `console' or a simple component number (e.g.,
            port or module number), such as `1', depending on the
            physical component naming syntax of the device.

            If there is no local name, or this object is otherwise not
            applicable, then this object contains a zero-length string.

            Note that the value of entPhysicalName for two physical
            entities will be the same in the event that the console
            interface does not distinguish between them, e.g., slot-1
            and the card in slot-1.

columns.name: entPhysicalHardwareRev
columns.description:
The vendor-specific hardware revision string for the
            physical entity.  The preferred value is the hardware
            revision identifier actually printed on the component itself
            (if present).

            Note that if revision information is stored internally in a





            non-printable (e.g., binary) format, then the agent must
            convert such information to a printable format, in an
            implementation-specific manner.

            If no specific hardware revision string is associated with
            the physical component, or this information is unknown to
            the agent, then this object will contain a zero-length
            string.

columns.name: entPhysicalFirmwareRev
columns.description:
The vendor-specific firmware revision string for the
            physical entity.

            Note that if revision information is stored internally in a
            non-printable (e.g., binary) format, then the agent must
            convert such information to a printable format, in an
            implementation-specific manner.

            If no specific firmware programs are associated with the
            physical component, or this information is unknown to the
            agent, then this object will contain a zero-length string.

tables.name: entLogicalEntry
tables.description:
Information about a particular logical entity.  Entities
            may be managed by this agent or other SNMP agents (possibly)
            in the same chassis.

columns.name: entLogicalDescr
columns.description:
A textual description of the logical entity.  This object
            should contain a string which identifies the manufacturer's
            name for the logical entity, and should be set to a distinct
            value for each version of the logical entity.

columns.name: entLogicalType
columns.description:
An indication of the type of logical entity.  This will
            typically be the OBJECT IDENTIFIER name of the node in the
            SMI's naming hierarchy which represents the major MIB
            module, or the majority of the MIB modules, supported by the
            logical entity.  For example:
               a logical entity of a regular host/router -> mib-2
               a logical entity of a 802.1d bridge -> dot1dBridge
               a logical entity of a 802.3 repeater -> snmpDot3RptrMgmt
            If an appropriate node in the SMI's naming hierarchy cannot
            be identified, the value 'mib-2' should be used.

columns.name: entLogicalCommunity
columns.description:
An SNMPv1 or SNMPv2C community-string which can be used to
            access detailed management information for this logical
            entity.  The agent should allow read access with this
            community string (to an appropriate subset of all managed
            objects) and may also return a community string based on the
            privileges of the request used to read this object.  Note
            that an agent may return a community string with read-only
            privileges, even if this object is accessed with a read-
            write community string. However, the agent must take care





            not to return a community string which allows more
            privileges than the community string used to access this
            object.

            A compliant SNMP agent may wish to conserve naming scopes by
            representing multiple logical entities in a single 'default'
            naming scope.  This is possible when the logical entities
            represented by the same value of entLogicalCommunity have no
            object instances in common.  For example, 'bridge1' and
            'repeater1' may be part of the main naming scope, but at
            least one additional community string is needed to represent
            'bridge2' and 'repeater2'.

            Logical entities 'bridge1' and 'repeater1' would be
            represented by sysOREntries associated with the 'default'
            naming scope.

            For agents not accessible via SNMPv1 or SNMPv2C, the value
            of this object is the empty string.  This object may also
            contain an empty string if a community string has not yet
            been assigned by the agent, or no community string with
            suitable access rights can be returned for a particular SNMP
            request.

            Note that this object is deprecated. Agents which implement
            SNMPv3 access should use the entLogicalContextEngineID and
            entLogicalContextName objects to identify the context
            associated with each logical entity.  SNMPv3 agents may
            return a zero-length string for this object, or may continue
            to return a community string (e.g., tri-lingual agent
            support).

columns.name: entLogicalTAddress
columns.description:
The transport service address by which the logical entity
            receives network management traffic, formatted according to
            the corresponding value of entLogicalTDomain.

            For snmpUDPDomain, a TAddress is 6 octets long, the initial
            4 octets containing the IP-address in network-byte order and
            the last 2 containing the UDP port in network-byte order.
            Consult 'Transport Mappings for Version 2 of the Simple
            Network Management Protocol' (RFC 1906 [RFC1906]) for
            further information on snmpUDPDomain.

columns.name: entLogicalTDomain
columns.description:
Indicates the kind of transport service by which the
            logical entity receives network management traffic.
            Possible values for this object are presently found in the
            Transport Mappings for SNMPv2 document (RFC 1906
            [RFC1906]).

tables.name: f3AmpConfigEntry
tables.description: A conceptual row in the f3AmpConfigTable.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigRole
columns.description: Configures AMP instance to be either Client or Server.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigProtocol
columns.description: Configures protocol over which AMP message will be transported.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigEnabled
columns.description: Enables or Disables AMP on a Port.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigPort
columns.description:
Port on which AMP Server or Client will operate. This port will 
          transmit/receive AMP messages. The AMP can operate on Access Ports and
          Network Port and f3AmpConfigPort shall point to either cmEthernetAccPortIndex 
          or cmEthernetNetPortIndex.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigStatus
columns.description: The Status of AMP.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigRemSysName
columns.description:
When f3AmpConfigRole is 'server' this object specifies sysName which is sent
          in a AMP message to be configfured on a AMP Client device.
          When f3AmpConfigRole is 'client' this object is read-only and shows the sysName
          value which was received from the AMP Server.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigRemSysIpAddr
columns.description:
When f3AmpConfigRole is 'server' this object specifies System IP address 
          which is sent in a AMP message to be configfured on a AMP Client device.
          When f3AmpConfigRole is 'client' this object is read-only and shows the 
          System IP address value which was received from the AMP Server.

columns.name: f3AmpConfigRemSysIpMask
columns.description:
When f3AmpConfigRole is 'server' this object specifies System IP mask 
          which is sent in a AMP message to be configfured on a AMP Client device.
          When f3AmpConfigRole is 'client' this object is read-only and shows the 
          System IP mask value which was received from the AMP Server.

columns.name: f3AmpStatsProvDataRx

columns.name: f3AmpStatsProvRequestTx
